activities.markdown:
# Chords Built From a Scale

Every note of a scale can be a chord root. In a major key the quality pattern is:

| Degree | Quality | In C major |
|--------|---------|------------|
| I | Major | C |
| ii | minor | Dm |
| iii | minor | Em |
| IV | Major | F |
| V | Major | G |
| vi | minor | Am |
| vii° | diminished | Bdim |

This explains why G–D–Em–C works so well — those are I, V, vi, IV in the key of G.

activities.question: In a major key, the ii chord is always which quality?
activities.explanation: In every major key, the chord built on the 2nd degree (ii) is always minor. In C major: D minor. In G major: A minor.
